notes: agy autonomous-loop ANALOGUE: no native /goal self-prompting loop, so the recorded shape is its within-one-turn multi-tool autonomous loop (the 69343e3e sample runs 17+ run_command cycles, self-correcting, with no user prompts between). Single-birth adapter (transcript-first, one <conv-id>). Because the iterations live inside ONE turn, the daemon sees a SINGLE continuous working span: ready→working on the prompt, then assistant_message+ToolUse / function_call_output cycles (all non-settling), then a single working→ready at the terminal empty PLANNER_RESPONSE — exactly the spec's strict 'no ready between internal iterations'. This is the best sibling fit (claudecode flickers per Stop-hook iteration; antigravity does not). No waiting under auto-approve. ≥2 internal tool cycles, final ready. Tokens/cost not in the JSONL → observations assert tokens_nonzero:false, cost_nonzero:false; no goal_status/iteration counter (not an agy construct); model omitted (provisional). INTERACTIVE driver. driver=gap:wait_turn — wait_turn (must NOT return on a mid-turn tool cycle) is a driver stub; record ports it + the launch_repl session-resolution SEAM first.

text: antigravity session (<conv-id>) appears in ready when transcript.jsonl is written
Seed prompt → working at loop start

Spec: state stays working for the ENTIRE autonomous run — the model runs its own sequential run_command cycles (>=2 internal iterations) with NO ready between them and no waiting (auto-approve); all intra-turn assistant_message/function_call_output are non-settling
Terminal empty PLANNER_RESPONSE when the goal is reached → turn_done → working→ready; final state ready
